danger_signals.visual_form: Fighter sprites on map screen; enemy ship models in action perspective
Missile paths displayed on strategic map moving toward Corneria
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent planetary damage; highest priority threat requiring player intercept

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy vessel symbols on map; spawns fighter squadrons visually
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat asset that generates additional waves of fighters

danger_signals.visual_form: Damage percentage rising on UI; visual planetary degradation implied
danger_signals.meaning: World-state deterioration; game over at 100% damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Shield gauge depleting for selected pilot; Arwing visual damage states
danger_signals.meaning: Selected pilot approaching incapacitation; death if gauge empties

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive enemy ship designs and pilot silhouettes
Special enemy units targeting General Pepper's satellite asset on map
danger_signals.meaning: Critical defensive infrastructure compromised; loss results in Corneria bombardment

safety_signals.visual_form: Strategic overhead view of Lylat system with paused game time
safety_signals.meaning: Player has tactical control and planning opportunity; not in real-time combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Pilot portrait displayed with active shield gauge on UI
safety_signals.meaning: Playable asset intact; can engage enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Satellite icon present on map; defensive cannon functionality available
safety_signals.meaning: Limited but active planetary defense active against incoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Map areas with no enemy indicators or missile trajectories
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical breathing room; areas safe from immediate attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Return to map screen after action sequence completion

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arwing ship designs; pilot character models (Fox, Falco, Peppy, Slippy, Miyu, Fay)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Battleships, fighter squadrons, IPBMs, planetary bases
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Widespread deployment across Lylat system; orbital bombardment assets

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinctive mercenary ship designs; boss encounter formations (Wolf O'Donnell, Leon Powalski, Pigma Dengar, Algy)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mercenary vessels appearing as special encounters; characteristically arrogant/aggressive AI behavior
General Pepper's satellite defensive installation; planet Corneria as protected asset

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Persistent enemy markers across Lylat system map; multiple simultaneous threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: IPBMs advance toward Corneria each turn; enemy fighters patrol assigned sectors; Star Wolf conducts opportunistic raids

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Damage meter displays escalating percentage; visual planetary impact accumulation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Countdown pressure; enemies press attacks; game over condition approaches at 100%

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from map perspective to 3D action perspective; enemy squad composition visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time dilation effect—action time slower than map time; allows continued threat advancement during player engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Satellite icon changes state/visual indicator on map; cannon redirected visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Satellite switches to hostile fire targeting Corneria instead of enemies; loss of defensive coverage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shield gauge depletes to zero; Arwing destruction animation; pilot removed from unit roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Selected pilot unavailable for remainder of game; swap to surviving pilot mandatory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map area displays no enemy icons or threat indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can traverse that sector without combat; tactical repositioning opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinctive large enemy formation; unique ship designs for named antagonists
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heightened enemy aggression; complex attack patterns; signature dialogue/characterization

environmental_storytelling.technique: Lylat system map as narrative geography
environmental_storytelling.example: Physical layout of contested planets and orbital zones communicates scale of Andross invasion; multiple simultaneous threats across map emphasizes overwhelm and urgency
Planet displayed as home world requiring protection; damage meter represents cumulative war attrition; loss condition (100% damage) frames Corneria as strategic objective both sides fight over
Large vessel spawning fighter squadrons visually demonstrates military hierarchy and command structure of Andross forces; battleships function as distributed command nodes
Missile paths displayed on map show Andross strategy of bombardment; player must intercept specific launch vectors to prevent damage, creating spatial puzzle of trajectory prediction
Six playable characters with distinct visual designs and roles (Fox as leader, Peppy as mentor, Miyu/Fay as new recruits) communicate Star Fox team hierarchy and narrative progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: General Pepper's satellite as allied infrastructure
environmental_storytelling.example: Satellite visual presence on map indicates Allied defensive capability; vulnerability to special enemy units that can hijack it demonstrates war as system of competing assets

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves toward assigned target on map; engages if player intercepts; advances toward Corneria if unopposed
Spawns fighter waves; maintains position; represents high-priority target that must be destroyed to reduce threat generation
Advances deterministically toward Corneria each turn; advances faster during action sequences than during map planning; represents existential threat
Engages player with complex attack patterns; displays signature personality in gameplay (arrogance, precision, deviousness); boss-tier tactical threat
Shoots down limited enemies per round; provides marginal but measurable planetary defense
Redirects fire toward Corneria; becomes hostile asset; must be recaptured or destroyed
Responds to player input with arcade-style flight controls; shield gauge depletes from enemy fire; can be swapped with partner pilot
Ship explodes; pilot dies; removed from playable roster permanently; forces swap to remaining pilot or triggers game over if last pilot
Appears as final boss encounter; represents culmination of all faction forces; defeat results in campaign victory

notes: Star Fox 2 employs a hybrid strategic-tactical communication system: the map screen provides world-state overview with paused strategy, while action sequences provide real-time danger feedback. The semi-real-time system (enemy advancement during player engagement) creates constant pressure communication. Faction identity is primarily behavioral rather than visual; Star Fox = player agency, Andross forces = systematic threat waves, Star Wolf = rival chaotic threat. Corneria damage meter serves as primary world-state metric. The satellite hijacking mechanic communicates warfare as control of infrastructure assets. Pilot death is permanent and irreversible within a playthrough, creating high-stakes resource management messaging.
